verdict.headline: DO NOT TRANSACT: This wallet is sanctioned (OFAC SDN / DPRK / Lazarus Group) and publicly identified as the Ronin Bridge Exploiter.
verdict.reasoning: Sanctions screening returned a confirmed match against OFAC SDN, the Lazarus Group, and DPRK programs — this is a hard veto that overrides all other signals. Web sentiment independently corroborates this: Etherscan publicly tags the address as the "Ronin Bridge Exploiter" tied to one of the largest crypto hacks on record. Even though onchain_history was unresolved and the labels/contract_analysis endpoints returned only API metadata, the sanctions hit plus corroborating web evidence is sufficient for a high-confidence negative verdict. Sending funds to this address would likely constitute a sanctions violation in addition to funding stolen-asset laundering.

findings.finding: Confirmed sanctions match on OFAC SDN, Lazarus Group, and DPRK lists (risk_level: high). Hard veto.
Etherscan and public reporting identify this address as the 'Ronin Bridge Exploiter' involved in the Ronin bridge hack; holds ~101.8 ETH (~$216k) across 429 transactions.
Labels endpoint returned only API metadata; no usable label payload, but consistent with sanctions/exploiter classification from other sources.
Address is an EOA (per web sentiment); contract analysis not applicable.
Onchain history unresolved by tooling, but web sentiment indicates 429 transactions and ~$216k balance — not a new wallet.

services.rationale: Covers the broadest set of crypto-specific sanctions lists (12,997+ addresses across OFAC SDN, USDT/USDC Blacklists, Ransomwhere, OpenSanctions, UK OFSI) at the lowest price tier ($0.001), making it the strongest wallet-focused sanctions screen available.
Directly classifies crypto addresses into actionable entity types (exchange, mixer, contract, known entity), which is the clearest match for wallet-labelling intent at a competitive $0.005 price.
Provides ETH & ERC20 balances and transaction history at the lowest price ($0.001) with a clean base endpoint, matching the category intent without narrowing to a single sub-resource path.
Searches across crypto-focused web sources for wallet/entity mentions, which is the best fit for surfacing web sentiment signals relevant to a wallet risk run.
Bundles a vulnerability scanner, risk scorer, and audit checklist in one AI-agent-oriented tool at a low $0.005 price, offering the most comprehensive contract analysis coverage for the cost.
